zh.lifeSummary: Aubreye de Vesci是Eustace fitz John和Beatrix de Vesci的女兒，出生在英國越可爾。她於大約1124年與Henry de Lacy Baron of Pontefract and Lord of Blackburnshire在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1175年之後在英國賀福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vesci
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Calabria): patronymic or plural form of   Vescio  .

variants.name: Vescio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Carmela, Ettore, Pasquale, Serafino, Vito.

Italian: probably a nickname from Calabrese dialect vesciu ‘cross-eyed person’ or vescia ‘kind of mushroom’. In Italy the surname is most frequent in Calabria, specifically in Lamezia Terme in Catanzaro province.

nameMeanings.name: Aubreye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

variants.name: Aubree
meanings: From a Norman French form of the Germanic name Alberic, from alb ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ric ‘power’. This was the name, according to Germanic mythology, of the king of the elves. The native Old English form, Ælfrīc, borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury, did not long survive the Conquest. Aubrey was a relatively common given name during the Middle Ages, but later fell out of favour. Its occurrence since the 19th century may in part represent a transferred use of the surname derived from the Norman given name, as well as a revival of the latter. In the United States, this is mainly used as a girl's name, perhaps under the influence of Audrey .

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Aubreye de Vesci was born in Yorkshire, England as the daughter of Eustace fitz John and Beatrix de Vesci. She married Henry de Lacy Baron of Pontefract and Lord of Blackburnshire about 1124, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died after 1175, in Herefordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Aubreye de Vesci est née à Everwyck, Angleterre, fille d’Eustace fitz John et Beatrix de Vesci. Elle a épousé Henry de Lacy Baron of Pontefract and Lord of Blackburnshire environ 1124, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1175, à Herefordshire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Aubreye de Vesci(은)는 영국 요르크실르에서 Eustace fitz John(와)과 Beatrix de Vesci의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1124년 영국에서 Henry de Lacy Baron of Pontefract and Lord of Blackburnshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1175년 이후 영국 허퍼드실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Aubreye de Vesci родилась в местности еркшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Eustace fitz John и Beatrix de Vesci. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de Lacy Baron of Pontefract and Lord of Blackburnshire около 1124 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1175 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия.
